title: hypericum rochelii subsp. pseudotenellum
intro: Hypericum rochelii subsp. pseudotenellum is a subspecies of the St. John's Wort plant, a flowering plant native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and five-lobed calyx. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Hypericum rochelii subsp. pseudotenellum include its potential to treat anxiety and depression, as well as its antioxidant and wound-healing properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are taken internally to promote overall well-being and reduce stress.
